## [00:00 - 03:02]

So, in this video, we're going to be going over Asana and essentially what all of these different tabs here mean, as well as then obviously on the left hand side, we'll see RCA as well as, you know, disqualified section two. So, jumping right into things, these are clients here. Team clients need to be onboarded or waiting. So essentially what happens whenever one of the STA closers or there's a new STA client that comes in that also paid for recruiting, Savanna or Jacob as well, whoever will come in here and put whatever their first name, you know, first and last name is and then how many hires they get. You see there's nothing else except for that. That's just for us to track here internally. And then down at the bottom, when clients are ready for recruiting and they're ready to start the recruiting process, they'll go ahead and fill out an application which will then auto populate into here. So, this is Tiffany, she's ready for application. So, her application is here and we just need to put together templates for her and all of that. This is the section here. From there, Savanna's active team clients. So, these are active clients that Savanna is currently recruiting for. Same thing here. These are Jacob's clients as well. Application responses are where all of the applications for sales reps auto populate into. So, obviously Jacob will go through or whoever will go through this section here and then obviously send out booking links and all that kind of stuff to book in for an interview. Before obviously send over the booking links, he then either messages these people up here and asks them to either send over a video or a voice note. If the application is a little bit iffy, we still think there could be some potential. We'll get them to send over a video, see how they do on camera. And then if the application is solid and they're good, we just get a voice note just to make sure that obviously everything sounds all good and they're all good on the phone. And they then get dropped down here while we're waiting for them to reply back with one of the two. From there, once we get something back from them, whether it's a video or the voice note, the person that's going through that will then send out the booking link if it makes sense. Then once they get sent out the booking link, the person gets put into this section here. And then from there, obviously the interviews will be booked. And then as soon as the interviews are obviously completed, then they'll be moved into their respective columns here. So this one here, we don't actually really use. So you can just forget about this one. Complete our interviews is where all of the setters go. So if you approve someone to be put into the setter pipeline, then you would put them into this pipeline here under setters. This is for the setter closure. So if you approve someone to be a setter closure, you'd put them under the setter closure column. From here, same thing with closers. If they're closing, they're good, close and have closing capability, you'd put them under the closing section here. Obviously, this is all explained in the differences between the three up above. This here, international reps, these are people that are in our pipeline that, whether they're

## [03:02 - 06:04]

a closure setter or a setter closure, they are put into this pipeline and we know that they're outside of the US. Obviously there's nothing wrong. Just for us, it's easier to have them in one section rather than having to look through every single section and try to find those people. These are all the international reps you can see here. Australia, Israel, Ireland, Australia, Bali, etc. These are all these people in here. Someone that you talk to is outside of the United States, but you obviously still approve them, then you would put them into this pipeline here. From there, high ticket savages only. So these are people that are absolute studs. People who in the online space, they've closed over $500,000 in cash collected over the past 12 months, you then put them into here. Usually, these are people that are closer, A minus, A, A plus. These are the people that sit in this range. These are probably the best of the best of our pipeline. From here, connected with client, these are just people that once Savanna or Jacob reaches out to the reps with the opportunity and the rep wants to get connected or is down to work on that or close on that or set on that opportunity. When they get connected to the client, they then get dropped into here so that we're not actively looking through this pipeline and trying to find them an opportunity if they're already connected with one of our clients. If for whatever reason they don't get hired or anything at all like that, they then get put back into one of these pipelines up here. If they are then connected with the client and they get approved and they get hired, then they either go down into this section here, which is Jacob's hired reps or Savanna's hired reps, depending on obviously which account it is. Then from there, this is the on-deck section. The on-deck section is usually people that were in our pipeline but then ended up taking another job, so ended up taking another role or just doing something else. You can see here mostly because they took another job, took another gig, part time only, took another gig. That kind of thing this guy here is playing soccer. The kind of people that are on-deck, we usually go through every couple of months, I think every quarter, and just reach out to all those people, see where they're at, see if we can put them back into our pipeline, see if things changed. From here, these are Savanna's accounts, so people that have successfully hired in the past, they came through, they needed recruiting and now they're all good, they don't need any more recruiting. Then same thing here, these are Jacob's accounts that don't need any more recruiting. From here, this is a disqualified rep, so if we then disqualified people, we then drop them down into this section here and then what will happen is once this section gets pretty filled up, then we just move these people into this disqualified section here within Asana. Then quit people, people that have pretty much just quit whatever opportunity they were on, they're fired or whatever the case may be and they're kind of in transition phase almost if you will. Usually, they reach out to them and see if we can put them back in the pipeline or see
